response: the fraction $\frac{100!}{10^{50}}$ is written as an irreducible fraction, meaning that the denominator is the product of the prime factors of the numerator that are not canceled out by the denominator. the denominator of this fraction will be the product of the prime factors of 100! that are not present in 10^{50}. the prime factors of 10 are 2 and 5, and since 10^{50} is $2^{50} \times 5^{50}$, the denominator will be the product of the prime factors of 100! that are not 2 or 5. the prime factors of 100! include many 2s and 5s, but the denominator will be the product of the remaining 2s and 5s in the prime factorization of 100! after dividing out the 2s and 5s in 10^{50}. the number of 2s in 100! is calculated as $\lfloor \frac{100}{2} \rfloor + \lfloor \frac{100}{4} \rfloor + \lfloor \frac{100}{8} \rfloor + \lfloor \frac{100}{16} \rfloor + \lfloor \frac{100}{32} \rfloor + \lfloor \frac{100}{64} \rfloor = 50 + 25 + 12 + 6 + 3 + 1 = 97$. the number of 5s in 100! is $\lfloor \frac{100}{5} \rfloor + \lfloor \frac{100}{25} \rfloor + \lfloor \frac{100}{125} \rfloor = 20 + 4 + 0 = 24$. the denominator will be the product of the remaining 2s and 5s after subtracting 50 2s and 50 5s. the remaining 2s are 97 - 50 = 47, and the remaining 5s are 24 - 50 = -26, which is not possible. this means that the denominator will have 47
